blocks.heading: The visual-atlas product — it scans a repo, groups modules into domains, drafts a two-level map, and renders `atlas.html` plus a page per domain.

rows.value: The atlas **block model** (the `AtlasBlock` union — `atlas-tldr`, `domain-map`, `domain-index`, `domain-tldr`, `components`, `depth`, `owns`, `seams`, `diagram-section`) and the `atlas.domains.json` **grouping config**.

A standing onboarding map: the atlas page gives the all-domains picture, each `domain-<slug>/` page is a deep-dive + reference. Mechanical scan handles the inventory; a human writes the prose and the *why*.
The page you are reading **is** this domain. The very generator documented below produced it — the scanner drafted these cards, then a human enriched them.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "Hybrid — the scanner does the inventory, the human writes the domains and the why."
bigIdea.sub: `scanInventory` finds modules, imports, and exports; `firstGuessConfig`/`reconcile` group them into domains from `atlas.domains.json` globs and report **drift** (never auto-applies it); `buildAtlasDraft`/`buildDomainDraft` emit a skeleton with empty `purpose`/`detail` fields. The draft is written **only when absent** (never clobbers authored prose unless `--force`), and `lint-atlas` flags the blank fields a human still needs to fill.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: One CLI (three modes) over five library modules: a grouping config with drift detection, a scanner that builds drafts, the block model that types the page, an assembler that renders HTML, and a completeness lint. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: CLI
cards.purpose: `bin/atlas.ts` — the entry with three modes: **full scan** (config + drift + draft-when-absent + render), **single-domain** (rescan one page), and **render-only** (`--all` / `--blocks`). Lays out `atlas.*` at the top and each domain in its own `domain-<slug>/` folder.

cards.name: config + drift
cards.purpose: `atlas-config.ts` — the human-owned `atlas.domains.json`: one domain per `globs` set. `firstGuessConfig` seeds it (one domain per srcRoot subdir); `reconcile` refills resolved `modules` and reports drift — unassigned modules, stale paths, empty domains.

cards.name: scan + draft
cards.purpose: `gather-atlas.ts` — walks srcRoots, parses imports/exports/routers per module, aggregates cross-domain edges, and emits the atlas + domain draft documents (with the mechanical domain-map diagram).

cards.name: block model
cards.purpose: `atlas-blocks.ts` — the `AtlasBlock` union that types every page, plus page-option shapes (`AtlasOpts`/`DomainOpts`), the layer-color table, and the id-uniqueness / diagram-collection helpers. **This very file is documented below.**

cards.name: render
cards.purpose: `assemble-atlas.ts` — turns a block list into a self-contained HTML page: `assembleAtlas` for the map, `assembleDomain` for a domain page. Renders each `diagram-section`/`depth` diagram via the diagrams domain.

cards.name: completeness lint
cards.purpose: `lint-atlas.ts` — the demo-standard floor. Warns (via `onWarn`, never errors) when a page skips the lead, the map, the index, the components, an internal-arch diagram when large, or the seams — and flags scanner-drafted tiles with no purpose.

blocks.title: How a full scan runs
blocks.intro: `gather-atlas` is the hub. The repo is scanned into an `Inventory`; `atlas-config` groups modules into domains (first-guess or reconcile, reporting drift); `buildAtlasDraft`/`buildDomainDraft` emit skeleton docs **only when absent**; a human enriches the blank `purpose`/`detail` fields; then `assembleAtlas`/`assembleDomain` render `atlas.html` + each `domain-<slug>/` page — with `lint-atlas` gating the completeness floor.

diagram.caption: Drift is **reported, never applied** — the human edits `atlas.domains.json` globs to resolve it.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece — what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the toolkit.

components.name: CLI
detail: The single entry point with three modes (paths may be relative — they resolve against the cwd). **Full scan** (`--repo <dir> --out <dir>`) loads or first-guesses `atlas.domains.json`, scans the repo, reconciles + reports drift, writes the config, drafts `atlas.json` and each `domain-<slug>/domain-<slug>.json` **only when absent** (or always with `--force`), then renders every doc. **Single-domain** (`--domain <slug>`) rescans and regenerates just that page (and prints a tile-update note). **Render-only** replays committed JSON — `--all <dir>` re-renders the atlas plus every `domain-<slug>/` folder, `--blocks <file>` renders one page.

`writeDraftIfAbsent` is the no-clobber guarantee: it skips any existing draft unless `--force`. The layout puts `atlas.{html,json}` + `atlas.domains.json` at the top of the out dir, and each domain self-contained in its own `domain-<slug>/` folder (page + diagram sidecars). It also warns about orphaned `domain-*/` folders left after a regroup — but never deletes files. Every mode except `--blocks` also refreshes `atlas-check.mjs` in the out dir (`emitChecker` copies the tool-owned `assets/atlas-check.mjs`, always overwritten) — the self-contained drift checker target repos commit and run from pre-commit/CI with plain Node.

exports.name: --repo
exports.desc: Full scan: config + drift + draft-when-absent + render.

exports.name: --domain
exports.desc: With `--repo`: rescan + regenerate one domain page.

exports.name: --all
exports.desc: Re-render the atlas + every `domain-<slug>/` from committed JSON.

exports.name: --blocks
exports.desc: Render one committed page JSON without rescanning.

exports.name: --force
exports.desc: Overwrite existing draft JSON (default: never clobber).

connections.body: `atlas.domains.json`, draft JSON (when absent), the rendered `.html`/`.json` per page, and `atlas-check.mjs` (drift checker, always overwritten)

components.name: config + drift
detail: `atlas.domains.json` is the human-owned source of truth: each `DomainConfig` carries a `slug`, `name`, and `globs` (the editable lever); the scanner fills the resolved `modules`. `firstGuessConfig` seeds a fresh config — one domain per immediate child directory of each srcRoot — so a new repo starts grouped, not blank. `reconcile` refills each domain's `modules` from its `globs` against the live inventory while **preserving every human-owned field**.

Crucially, `reconcile` reports **drift** but never applies it: `newModules` (in the repo, matched by no glob), `stalePaths` (config paths no longer present), and `emptyDomains` (globs resolving to nothing). The human resolves drift by editing globs. `matchGlob` is the tiny anchored matcher — `**` spans segments, `*` spans within one.

exports.name: firstGuessConfig()
exports.desc: Seeds a config: one domain per srcRoot subdir.

exports.name: reconcile()
exports.desc: → `{ config, drift }` — refills modules, reports drift, never auto-applies.

exports.name: matchGlob()
exports.desc: The anchored glob matcher — `**` spans segments, `*` within one.

connections.body: `atlas.domains.json` — the grouping config the human edits

components.name: scan + draft
detail: The hub of the [full-scan flow](#arch). `scanInventory` walks the srcRoots, and per module records resolved in-repo import keys, exported names, and whether it is a tRPC router — skipping generated/test trees and co-located `*.test`/`*.spec` files. It also collects Prisma model names. `aggregateDomainEdges` rolls module→module imports up to cross-domain slug→slug edges (intra-domain edges dropped).

`buildAtlasDraft` emits the atlas-page doc: a tile per domain (with file counts and dep edges) plus the mechanical `domainMapDiagram`. `buildDomainDraft` groups a domain's modules by subdirectory into component cards + deep sections, listing real files and exports — but leaves every `purpose`/`detail` field **blank** for the human. The scanner never writes prose.

connections.body: [config](#c-config) — the grouped `AtlasConfig` to index modules to domains
the draft docs the [CLI](#c-cli) writes when absent

components.name: block model
detail: The type backbone of every atlas page — and the file this very section documents. The `AtlasBlock` union has nine members: atlas-page blocks (`atlas-tldr`, `domain-map`, `domain-index`), domain-page blocks (`domain-tldr`, `components`, `depth`, `owns`, `seams`), and the shared `diagram-section`. `ComponentDeep` is one expandable depth section; `AtlasDiagram` is a rendered d2/mermaid pair.

Beyond the types it carries page-option shapes (`AtlasOpts`/`DomainOpts`), `LAYER_DOTS` (the layer → fill;stroke color table), and small helpers: `collectAtlasDiagrams` pulls every rendered diagram in document order, `assertUniqueAtlasIds` enforces unique block/component/diagram ids, and `isAtlasChapter`/`atlasChapterLabel` drive the numbered chapter nav.
components.codeHtml:
export type AtlasBlock =
  | AtlasTldrBlock | DomainMapBlock | DomainIndexBlock
  | DomainTldrBlock | ComponentsBlock | DepthBlock
  | OwnsBlock | SeamsBlock
  | DiagramSectionBlock;

files.name: atlas-blocks.ts
files.desc: The `AtlasBlock` union, `AtlasDiagram`/`ComponentDeep`, opts, `LAYER_DOTS`, and the id/diagram helpers.

exports.name: AtlasBlock
exports.desc: The nine-member block union every page is a list of.

exports.name: collectAtlasDiagrams()
exports.desc: Every rendered diagram, in document order.

connections.body: the atlas block model — every [domain](#c-gather) draft and [render](#c-assemble) is typed by it

components.name: render
detail: Turns a block list into a self-contained HTML page. `assembleAtlas` renders the map page (lead, domain-map, tile index); `assembleDomain` renders a domain page (lead, components, internal-arch, depth, owns, seams). `renderAtlasBlock` dispatches per block type, `renderAtlasDiagram` emits each diagram's d2/mermaid render with its authored legend, and `atlasLegend` builds the legend rows.

Every diagram is rendered through the diagrams domain up front (collected via `collectAtlasDiagrams`), then looked up by id while rendering. The output reuses the review app-shell/CSS from the recap domain, so atlas pages share the toolkit's look. The CLI calls these two functions and writes the HTML next to the page JSON.

exports.name: renderAtlasDiagram()
exports.desc: Per-diagram render: svg box + legend + caption.

components.name: completeness lint
detail: The demo-standard floor, mirroring the spec/recap linters: heuristics surfaced via `onWarn`, never hard errors. `lintAtlas` checks the atlas page leads with a `atlas-tldr` 'Start here', shows a domain map, and has a `domain-index` whose tiles all have a real `purpose` — flagging any blank ones the scanner left.

`lintDomain` checks a domain page opens with a `domain-tldr`, lists its `components`, includes an internal-arch `diagram-section` when it has four-plus deep components (`LARGE_COMPONENTS`), and names its `seams`. The warnings tell the human exactly which scanner-drafted fields still need enriching — closing the hybrid loop.

exports.name: lintAtlas()
exports.desc: → warnings: missing lead / map / index, tiles with no purpose.

exports.name: lintDomain()
exports.desc: → warnings: missing lead / components / arch-when-large / seams.

connections.body: the [block model](#c-blocks) — inspects block types and tile purposes

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Atlas owns the page block model and the grouping config — the two contracts every atlas page is built from. The block types below are the shape of a page; `AtlasConfig`/`atlas.domains.json` is the human-owned grouping.

rows.name: AtlasConfig · atlas.domains.json
rows.desc: The human-owned grouping: domains as `slug`/`name`/`globs`, with scanner-resolved `modules`. The one lever for which files form which domain.

rows.name: atlas-tldr · domain-map · domain-index
rows.desc: The atlas-page blocks: the 'Start here' lead, the all-domains map, and the grid of domain tiles.

rows.name: domain-tldr · components · depth · owns · seams
rows.desc: The domain-page blocks: the lead, the component cards, the in-depth sections, the data-owned table (this block), and the neighbor seams.

rows.name: diagram-section
rows.desc: The shared rendered-diagram block — the atlas spine/map and a domain page's internal-arch.

rows.name: Inventory · ModuleInfo · Drift
rows.desc: The mechanical scan products: per-module imports/exports/router flags, Prisma models, and the reported config drift.

blocks.note: The block model lives in `atlas-blocks.ts`; the config types in `atlas-config.ts`. The scanner fills `modules` and the draft skeletons; a human owns `globs`, `purpose`, and `detail`.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: Exposes the atlas CLI and its block model; depends on the rest of the toolkit for parsing, rendering, diagrams, and the shared app-shell.

exposes.note: full scan: config + drift + draft + render
the page contract authored pages target

blocks.note: `gather-atlas` imports **code-intel** (`imports`/`dep-graph`/`trpc-parse`/`prisma-schema`) for the scan; `atlas-blocks` builds on **core**'s `DiagramBlock`/`DiagramKind`; `assemble-atlas` renders via **diagrams** and reuses the **recap** review app-shell + `review.css`. The dependency on the rest of the toolkit is by reuse, not control — atlas stays the driver.
